167277.explanation: Nederweert is already attractive to entrepreneurs due to location, space, and entrepreneurship. Investing in good facilities, accessibility, and suitable business parks is more effective than launching separate promotion campaigns.

167278.explanation: CDA Nederweert finds it important that developments around Ospeldijk are carefully weighed. The area should not be rigidly fixed so that in the future there remains room for suitable developments with attention to landscape, surroundings, and agricultural interests.

167279.explanation: 'Always' is too absolute. Farmers must have space for side activities, such as a shop or campsite, provided this fits within spatial planning and the rural character. Entrepreneurial freedom is important, with oversight to prevent uncontrolled growth and taking surroundings into account.

167280.explanation: The CDA supports joint initiatives for sustainable energy. The municipality can provide targeted, temporary financial support to feasible projects as a starting impulse. Subsidies are not a goal in themselves; initiatives must be able to operate independently and economically responsible in the long term.

167283.explanation: Good integration strengthens the community. Targeted investments in language, work, and social interaction pay off and prevent problems later.

167284.explanation: CDA Nederweert finds decent housing for foreign workers important. A requirement that employers arrange this themselves has drawbacks, such as an overly dependent relationship. We prefer good, independent housing with clear rules and supervision.

167286.explanation: Sustainable entrepreneurship is important and encouraged. At the same time, an absolute exclusion of companies is not realistic or desirable. CDA chooses clear sustainability ambitions and development paths, challenging and supporting companies to become more sustainable.

167289.explanation: Targeted camera surveillance can increase safety in rural areas, provided it is applied carefully and with attention to privacy and the right locations.

167291.explanation: A substantial share of social rental housing is needed for starters and people with lower incomes. The CDA aims for about 30%, with attention to customization per location and a good balance with other types of housing.

167292.explanation: Height is possible at suitable locations and if it fits the environment. This way we use space more efficiently without compromising livability and the village character.

167293.explanation: We oppose a general obligation to owner-occupy. People should also be able to rent out their homes, for example to starters. However, we support an owner-occupancy obligation in specific cases, such as starter homes, to prevent speculation and keep homes available for the intended target group.

167295.explanation: Room rental can contribute to more housing, especially for people with lower incomes or small households. CDA Nederweert is therefore against a ban but does want clear rules to prevent abuses and nuisance.

167296.explanation: The CDA opposes an automatic increase of the income limit. Poverty assistance should target those who need it most. Customization is essential so support remains purposeful and effective and reaches those truly in need.

167297.explanation: CDA Nederweert finds it important that people on social assistance gain perspective on work as soon as possible. Many already contribute socially, for example through volunteer work or informal care. Therefore, a general obligation is not necessary; customization works better.

167298.explanation: CDA Nederweert finds it important that everyone contributes according to ability. At the same time, residents with financial problems should receive appropriate support to structurally get out of difficulties. Targeted help works better than general waivers.

167300.explanation: CDA Nederweert believes sufficient charging points for electric cars are important. Therefore, where possible, we want to gradually increase the number of parking spaces with charging stations, considering usage and parking pressure.

167301.explanation: CDA Nederweert considers traffic safety important. A general reduction to 30 km/h in the entire built-up area is not always effective and can have undesirable effects on accessibility and emergency services.

167303.explanation: CDA Nederweert values good involvement of residents. That is a task for the government and municipal council, with recognizable and approachable council members. Residents can be involved in various ways, but a fixed randomly selected advisory group adds no value for us.

167304.explanation: CDA Nederweert finds traffic safety important. A general speed limit of 60 km/h outside built-up areas is not always effective and can affect accessibility and emergency services. Tailored measures per road, fitting use and surroundings, are therefore better.

167306.explanation: CDA Nederweert finds it important that associations and other users of De Pinnenhof are seriously involved in the plans. Their wishes carry weight but are not exclusively decisive. Decisions must also consider feasibility, affordability, and the public interest, especially when previous designs proved much more expensive than budgeted.

167307.explanation: CDA Nederweert believes the development of De Pinnenhof should be viewed with an open and forward-looking perspective. The interests of users are paramount. If there is a more suitable location elsewhere in Nederweert, those options should not be excluded in advance.

167308.explanation: CDA Nederweert finds it important to help farmers make their businesses more sustainable. Targeted support can contribute to future-proof agriculture, provided it is feasible and offers perspective for the farm.

167310.explanation: CDA Nederweert supports the construction of a bicycle bridge over the Randweg Zuid to improve traffic safety. Cofinancing by the province is important, and the bridge must fit within a broader approach to accessibility and reducing traffic pressure.

167311.explanation: The CDA wants to remove through traffic from the center of Nederweert to improve safety and livability. A bypass can help with this, provided it is carefully integrated and developed with a future-oriented approach.

167312.explanation: CDA Nederweert is open to bicycle streets but opposes a general expansion. Bicycle streets do not contribute to traffic safety everywhere. Each location must be assessed to determine if a bicycle street demonstrably enhances safety and livability.

167314.explanation: The CDA opposes designating the area around the canal crossroads solely for recreation and tourism. That is too rigid. We want to look with an open mind at suitable developments, considering the landscape, environment, and quality.

167315.explanation: CDA Nederweert believes it is important to make lily cultivation more sustainable and reduce the use of pesticides. However, a general cultivation ban is currently legally untenable.

167320.explanation: CDA Nederweert is against a classic, large-scale industrial estate between the A2 and the village. Developments in this area must be carefully considered and fit the environment and livability.

167322.explanation: CDA Nederweert wants to work ambitiously on sustainability and reducing emissions. At the same time, goals must be achievable and consistent with what a municipality can manage. Therefore, we choose realistic steps instead of absolute targets.

167323.explanation: Language is the key to participation in society. By investing in additional language lessons, we increase opportunities for work, self-reliance, and participation.

167326.explanation: CDA Nederweert finds it important to build for all target groups: the elderly, starters, and families. Housing forms for the elderly belong to this, but not at the expense of other groups. We choose balance in housing development.

167328.explanation: CDA Nederweert believes local entrepreneurs should be given room to grow. At the same time, entrepreneurs from outside can also add value. Not origin, but whether a company fits Nederweert should be leading.

167329.explanation: CDA Nederweert wants to contribute to the development of the Brainport region because it offers opportunities for work and the economy. We do this in a way that suits Nederweert, preserving our identity and livability.

167331.explanation: CDA Nederweert finds it important that people with care needs quickly reach the right organization. Care often requires customization. One central desk for all care can actually complicate this. Clear routes and good guidance work better.

167277.explanation: JAN focuses on local business activity for local entrepreneurs instead of attracting outsiders.

167278.explanation: JAN wants to provide space for the housing needs of Ospeldijk and the preservation and development of the De Groote Peel nature reserve.

167279.explanation: JAN sees opportunities for collaboration between the municipality and partners from the leisure economy: for example, farm campsites, pick-your-own gardens, educational farm projects, or regional products for tourism.

167280.explanation: JAN finds it important that more energy and heat are stored. The municipality supports initiatives such as thermal energy storage (WKO) or neighborhood batteries and encourages the formation of energy communities.

167283.explanation: JAN believes everyone counts and participates. There are sufficient resources, but these must be deployed more targeted and based on need.

167284.explanation: The responsibility for housing does not lie solely with employers. Other parties can also offer living space. It is especially important that the municipality focuses on small-scale, well-organized housing for foreign workers, appropriate to local capacity.

167286.explanation: JAN supports the importance of sustainability but prefers to facilitate rather than restrict local entrepreneurs.

167289.explanation: Camera surveillance should not be used everywhere indiscriminately. It should be applied specifically in places where unsafe situations occur, respecting residents' privacy.

167291.explanation: JAN wants to build plenty of homes, also for people with a low income.

167292.explanation: JAN opts for suitable and small-scale housing that fits the village center. According to JAN, high-rise buildings are allowed only where their height and scale match the surrounding buildings and do not cause nuisance to the environment.

167293.explanation: JAN wants to introduce purchase protection for affordable homes so that these are not bought up by investors but remain available for local residents.

167295.explanation: JAN considers it especially important that people have suitable housing. The municipality should ensure decent and appropriate policies rather than a general ban on renting out single rooms.

167296.explanation: JAN wants to prevent residents of Nederweert from living in poverty by supporting them with income-increasing measures and providing appropriate support to overcome poverty situations.

167297.explanation: JAN wants everyone in our municipality to count and participate according to their ability. Depending on the person’s possibilities, with or without support, participation in day activities, volunteer work, or (where necessary adapted) paid work should be possible.

167298.explanation: JAN wants to stand up specifically for people with a low income.

167300.explanation: JAN wants to expand the number of charging stations (for both cars and bikes) in all centers and stimulate the use of sustainable vehicles such as electric cars and bikes.

167301.explanation: JAN wants to keep the current 30 km/h zones as they are. Arterial roads will be maintained at 50 km/h.

167303.explanation: JAN values advice from, among others, village councils, the Social Domain Advisory Council, the Disability Platform, and the Heritage Commission. These are actively considered in decisions on various themes.

167304.explanation: Assess and configure each situation according to what is appropriate for traffic safety.

167306.explanation: JAN wants the new Multi-Functional Accommodation De Pinnenhof to become a sustainable home based on the wishes and cooperation of associations, residents, the library, and social partners such as the youth health consultation office.

167307.explanation: JAN wants to realize new construction for MFA De Pinnenhof in Nederweert with a stage function, on the current Pinnenhof location.

167308.explanation: The community does not need to financially support farms in sustainability efforts. Tax money should be spent on general facilities and not on specific companies. Furthermore, sustainability should happen through national regulations and entrepreneurs’ own responsibility.

167310.explanation: JAN supports building a bicycle bridge over Randweg Zuid (N275, near McDonald's) to improve accessibility and traffic safety for cyclists.

167311.explanation: The Province and the municipality of Nederweert are investing in better traffic safety and flow on current provincial roads. The N266-N275 project has been established, including measures to improve current infrastructure. This will be implemented in the coming years.

167312.explanation: JAN finds it important to increase the safety of pedestrians and cyclists. Together with the municipality, it can be explored where it is possible and desirable to designate streets as bicycle streets.

167314.explanation: JAN opposes container transshipment at the canal crossroads but supports tourism and recreational opportunities in this beautiful area, including space for education about past events here.

167315.explanation: For JAN, health comes first, which means agricultural land should not be used for crops treated with harmful pesticides.

167320.explanation: JAN wants a nice entrance to Nederweert but no industrial estate.

167322.explanation: The national government decides on this, not the municipality.

167323.explanation: JAN also wants to pay more attention to better reaching and personally supporting people who have great difficulty with reading and writing. This concerns about 10% of our residents: often 65+, but also young people.

167326.explanation: JAN struggles with the word 'priority', because every resident, regardless of life stage or housing need, should be able to find a suitable and affordable home in the municipality of Nederweert.

167328.explanation: JAN gives priority to local entrepreneurs when establishing themselves in business parks.

167329.explanation: JAN believes the identity of the municipality of Nederweert should be preserved and focuses on the growth of our own centers, instead of growing along with the Brainport region.

167331.explanation: JAN believes residents should be able to go to a single care desk, but acknowledges that implementation depends on cooperation with other care parties and legal frameworks at multiple government levels.

parties.name: VVD
parties.fullName: Volkspartij voor Vrijheid en Democratie Nederweert

167277.explanation: The VVD considers it important that there is employment and lively activity. For that, we need suitable companies and innovation, and it's acceptable to spend money to attract good companies.

167278.explanation: A gentle transition from housing in the core, through agriculture, to nature.

167279.explanation: The municipality of Nederweert does not need dozens of campsites or shops in the rural area. We find it important that farmers can undertake business, but we choose quality. That means we cannot automatically allow everything; we do things in consultation.

167280.explanation: Everyone is free to develop sustainability projects. Sustainability projects must be viable, also without public money.

167283.explanation: We assume newcomers want to integrate into our community and will undertake activities themselves for this. Newcomers are also welcome to use all amenities in our municipality and meet people.

167284.explanation: We expect entrepreneurs to provide decent housing, livability, and compliance with rules, so that it remains pleasant for everyone to live here.

167286.explanation: We do not want heavy industry, but not every company has to win sustainability awards to be allowed to do business in our municipality and provide local jobs.

167289.explanation: If necessary, this can contribute to protecting the countryside and increasing the sense of security.

167291.explanation: The VVD finds it important that construction takes place. Imposing extra rules does not help with that.

167292.explanation: Many homes must be built; high-rise buildings are not taboo for us.

167293.explanation: It is important that, besides owner-occupied homes, sufficient rental homes remain available. If landlords are forced to occupy or sell their property, this reduces the rental market. What matters is that the home is occupied and not who owns it, with the protection renters deserve.

167295.explanation: People urgently seeking housing or wanting to live near family due to informal care also deserve their own place.

167296.explanation: The VVD wants to stimulate work, so people have a stable income and contribute to society.

167297.explanation: It is very normal to give something back to the community when you receive benefits.

167298.explanation: Residents who really cannot afford it must be helped. Debt forgiveness is a good instrument for this.

167300.explanation: Additional charging points in public spaces come at the expense of freely usable parking spaces.

167301.explanation: We want people to be able to get from A to B quickly, easily, and safely in our rural municipality. It is really not necessary to reduce the speed limit to 30 km/h everywhere.

167303.explanation: We appreciate residents' involvement. Participation takes place in projects, and we do not want to limit this to a group chosen by lottery.

167304.explanation: We want people to be able to get from A to B quickly, easily, and safely in our rural municipality. It is really not necessary to lower the speed limit to 60 km/h everywhere.

167306.explanation: Users know better than anyone what they need for their activities. It is very normal to come to a suitable arrangement based on their wishes.

167307.explanation: We want to make swift progress on De Pinnenhof. Currently, there are no other feasible locations, so we must move forward at this location.

167308.explanation: It is good if agricultural entrepreneurs want to make their business more sustainable, but as far as the VVD is concerned, it is not the municipality's role to finance this.

167310.explanation: A bicycle crossing with traffic lights is a safe traffic measure. Traffic figures confirm this. A crossing with a bridge is an expensive intervention and, as far as we are concerned, not effective in this situation.

167311.explanation: This is the only real solution to remove the large (freight) traffic flow from the heart of Nederweert and safely connect Budschop and Nederweert. We want to realize this quickly.

167312.explanation: Bicycle streets can be realized when it demonstrably contributes to safe roads, good flow for all road users, and the infrastructure allows it.

167314.explanation: The canal crossroads is a special area that we want to develop in an appropriate way so that even more people can enjoy this park-like environment.

167315.explanation: If lily cultivation can become more environmentally friendly, we are in favor of it.

167320.explanation: Entrepreneurs deserve the space to take the next step. The Nederweert VVD opts for an area with business activity for local entrepreneurs and leisure for residents such as a cinema, sports store, climbing hall, and hotel expansion.

167322.explanation: Policy must be realistic and feasible. That is why the VVD does not hold on to 2050.

167323.explanation: The Nederweert VVD considers language proficiency a primary personal responsibility. Additionally, there are already various accessible social activities to improve language and speaking skills.

167326.explanation: Giving priority to housing forms for the elderly, such as informal care homes, ensures better mobility in the housing market. This creates more homes available for all Nederweert residents.

167328.explanation: Our local entrepreneurs deserve to be able to do business at a scale that suits them. Both our growing and local starting entrepreneurs.

167329.explanation: The expansion of the Brainport region has been underway for some time. The choices we make now determine whether Nederweert becomes a municipality that dares to look ahead or one where developments happen to us. We clearly choose the former. A growing municipality Nederweert is an investment in strengthening our own amenities, while preserving our identity.

167331.explanation: Care in Nederweert means that people keep as much control over their lives as possible. This also means a service-oriented government that is understandable, approachable, and decisive. One care desk helps with this.

167277.explanation: We are not in favor of attracting more production and logistics companies (large distribution centers) to Nederweert. We find more companies related to recreation, tourism, and retail acceptable.

167278.explanation: There must be a buffer zone around the De Groote Peel nature reserve that includes nature-inclusive agriculture.

167279.explanation: DNA allows room for alternative income models, but these must be appropriate for the specific location. Not everything can be done everywhere.

167280.explanation: The municipality should encourage this but not contribute financially.

167283.explanation: People who come here must be able to quickly make a positive contribution to society. Therefore, they should learn the language as soon as possible.

167284.explanation: Employers are responsible for providing decent, quality housing for their workers at their company locations. It is not intended that houses are bought up to accommodate foreign workers.

167286.explanation: That is very logical to us.

167289.explanation: Safety in rural areas is important. But placing cameras everywhere is not the solution. We must also consider effective measures and respect residents' privacy.

167291.explanation: We can support this and the municipality has already agreed to this in the Housing Deal.

167292.explanation: This is certainly possible, but only in the right locations. The development at Staat is a good example in our opinion.

167293.explanation: It is not intended that 'slumlords' buy up homes.

167295.explanation: This is undesirable because these houses need to remain available for the housing market. However, we want targeted exceptions, such as for PSW (an organization for assisted living and day care in the region).

167296.explanation: Nederweert already has ample and good schemes, but they are still too little known. We need to increase awareness so more people can use them, with extra attention for arrangements for children.

167297.explanation: Those who can should receive a tailored solution. People's well-being is central.

167298.explanation: The municipality should not stop with this.

167300.explanation: There must be a good balance between charging stations and parking spaces.

167301.explanation: Safety comes first, and slow traffic users (cyclists and pedestrians) must be protected.

167303.explanation: It is important to involve residents in decision-making that affects everyone.

167304.explanation: Safety comes first, and slow traffic users (cyclists and pedestrians) must be protected. We align with the municipalities around us in this.

167306.explanation: The wishes of associations are extremely important in the new construction of De Pinnenhof since they are the users. However, the municipality must also make a good balance with other interests.

167307.explanation: The best location should emerge from the ongoing study. We are not bound to one location or another.

167308.explanation: Companies can and should pay for this themselves, just like other entrepreneurs do.

167310.explanation: This will improve the safety of cyclists going to Weert and the flow of cars on the N275. Both are urgently needed.

167311.explanation: There must be a direct connection between the A2 and the N266 well north of Nederweert. In our view, this is explicitly not the 2019 plan for a ring road close to the village, which was rejected after thorough research.

167312.explanation: In a bicycle street, cars are guests-they are allowed to be there. We improve the safety of cyclists and pedestrians.

167314.explanation: The canal crossroads is a unique area and, in our view, ideal for recreation and tourism. We also support the establishment of scouting in this area.

167315.explanation: Lily cultivation makes heavy use of pesticides that are harmful to public health. We also believe these agricultural lands can be better used for food production.

167320.explanation: This location is not suitable for an industrial estate with manufacturing and/or logistics companies. We continue to advocate for a park between Randweg West and the village.

167322.explanation: Climate is an important issue. We cannot afford not to pursue this for the sake of our (grand)children.

167323.explanation: Many people have low literacy and struggle with reading and writing. These are important skills to participate in society. We must help these people improve their language skills. The same applies to digital skills.

167326.explanation: Housing for seniors and starters has extra priority. We must therefore ensure good housing projects tailored to these groups in all centers.

167328.explanation: We want to support local entrepreneurs. Creating industrial estates for companies from outside the municipality is not the intention.

167329.explanation: The Eindhoven region is growing rapidly. Regarding housing and recreational facilities, Nederweert can certainly play an important role, but not in expanding with additional industrial areas.

167331.explanation: Residents must be able to turn to one care desk. This prevents people from getting lost in a maze of organizations and helps care organizations collaborate better.

statements.title: More buildings of five or more storeys should be allowed in Nederweert.
statements.pro: By building upwards, we make better use of space in Nederweert.
statements.con: High-rise buildings undermine the village character of Nederweert.

statements.title: The Pinnenhof should remain at its current location.
statements.moreInfo: De Pinnenhof is a community center located behind the church in Nederweert. De Pinnenhof is to become a multifunctional facility (MFA), with space for a library, activities by associations, and a place where residents can go for meeting and support.
statements.pro: The current location is central and close to other facilities. There are currently no other suitable locations. This debate has been going on for years. Investigating alternative locations would cost extra time and money.
statements.con: The plans for new construction at the current site are too expensive. We must first investigate whether other locations are suitable. The interests of the centre’s users come first. The location does not matter.

statements.title: There should be no business park built between the A2 and the village of Nederweert.
statements.pro: There should be a park on this site. That is better for the livability of Nederweert. There are already enough business parks in Nederweert and the surrounding area.
statements.con: Entrepreneurs in Nederweert should be given more space to grow. The arrival of new companies is good for the local economy.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in the surrounding countryside.

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.
Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities may use cameras to supervise public order. The police may then view and use the footage. In Nederweert there are currently cameras on the Pannenweg II industrial estate. This is organized by park management itself.
statements.pro: Camera surveillance can prevent unsafe situations, vandalism, and nuisance. It can help tackle illegal dumping of waste.
statements.con: Camera surveillance is an infringement on residents’ privacy. It is better to provide more police officers or BOAs (municipal enforcement officers) on the streets.

statements.title: The municipality should prioritize building housing for the elderly, such as homes for informal caregiving.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet voorrang geven aan het bouwen van woonvormen voor ouderen, zoals

titleParts.glossary: A self-contained dwelling on the grounds of another house, where, for example, grandparents can be cared for.

statements.pro: More housing for the elderly contributes to better flow in the housing market. This frees up more homes for first-time buyers and families.
statements.con: We should also build for first-time buyers and families. Building senior housing does not take priority.

statements.title: A bypass should be built around the village of Nederweert.
statements.pro: The existing roads N266 and N275 (Southern Ring Road) are currently far too busy. A ring road would make Nederweert safer and more livable. There would then be less through (freight) traffic through the village.
statements.con: We should adapt the N266 and N275 (Southern Ring Road) so traffic can flow better there. There should be an additional road between the A2 and the N266 well north of Nederweert. No ring road right next to the village.

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in the municipality of Nederweert should be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in de gemeente Nederweert moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.pro: We need social rental housing for young people, single households, and people with lower incomes.
statements.con: The municipality should not impose extra rules on housing construction. That increases the risk that less will be built.

statements.title: The area around Ospeldijk should remain agricultural land.
statements.moreInfo: Farmers can receive money from the national government and the province to convert agricultural land into nature. This mainly applies to farmland that borders nature reserves. The municipality decides together with the province where new nature is desired. For this the municipality must amend the Environmental Plan (Omgevingsplan).
statements.pro: Agricultural land provides a good transition between the village of Nederweert with housing and the De Groote Peel nature reserve. It is a kind of buffer zone. Farmers should remain in the rural area around Nederweert.
statements.con: The municipality should not fix the zoning of the area. There must be room for new plans in the future. In Ospeldijk there is mainly a need for housing.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for their foreign workers themselves.
statements.moreInfo: Labor migrants work in Nederweert: foreign workers who work here (temporarily). They mainly work in agriculture and horticulture. The municipality can set rules about where labor migrants may live and under what conditions.
statements.pro: We must prevent houses in residential neighborhoods from being bought up to house large numbers of foreign workers. Therefore employers should provide accommodation at or near their own company.
statements.con: It is not good if employees depend on their employer for their housing.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.moreInfo: Homeowners can install solar panels on their roof if the roof is suitable, or apply for a permit for a wind turbine in the garden if it is large enough. If that is not possible, people can buy or rent land together with others for one or more wind turbines or solar panels so they can still invest in renewable energy. Renewable energy is generated from sources that never run out, such as sunlight and wind.
statements.pro: Initiatives from residents are indispensable for the transition to sustainable energy. Municipal funding can provide a kick-start for initiatives that can eventually operate independently.
statements.con: The municipality should encourage sustainability, but does not need to spend money on it. Sustainability projects must be viable without subsidies.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.moreInfo: The built-up area is the part of the town or village where people live, work and shop. A speed limit of 50 km/h often applies there. On some streets this is already 30 km/h.
statements.pro: A 30 km/h speed limit makes traffic safer for pedestrians and cyclists. The number of traffic casualties will therefore decrease.
statements.con: This speed limit should apply in residential neighbourhoods, but not on larger through-roads within the built-up area. For traffic flow, a higher speed is better there.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a promotional campaign for Nederweert as an attractive location for businesses.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements on, for example, billboards, television, and social media.
The location where a company establishes itself.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities can attract companies by showing what they have to offer. This can be done, for example, with a promotional campaign. The goal is to convince companies to settle in the municipality.
statements.pro: The arrival of more companies brings life to the town and a vibrant local economy. New entrepreneurs bring innovation. For residents of Nederweert it is important that there are enough jobs close to home.
statements.con: The municipality should support local entrepreneurs rather than spend money attracting companies from outside. We should spend money on good amenities, business parks and infrastructure instead of a promotional campaign. Nederweert is already attractive enough for entrepreneurs.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality determines how many charging points for electric cars there are and where they are located. At the moment there are about 72 public charging points in Nederweert.
statements.pro: The municipality should encourage the use of sustainable vehicles.
statements.con: Additional charging points come at the expense of regular parking spaces, creating a shortage.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.pro: It is important that newcomers learn Dutch as quickly as possible. Then they can participate in society and make a positive contribution. Good integration of newcomers strengthens the Nederweert community and prevents problems later on.
statements.con: Newcomers who want to integrate should make the effort themselves. They can organise activities themselves. The municipality already spends enough on newcomer integration; that money could be used better and more targeted.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no work and no other benefit can apply to the municipality for social assistance. In some municipalities they are required to perform a counter-performance. They then do socially useful work for which they are not paid.
statements.pro: It is perfectly normal to give something back when you receive benefits. Everyone in the municipality is included, participates, and contributes to society according to their ability.
statements.con: A requirement is not necessary. Many people already do this, for example as volunteers or as informal caregivers. Not everyone can work.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality can require people who buy a house to live in it themselves. They may then not rent the house to others. In some municipalities the owner may have their adult children or parents live in the house.
statements.pro: We must prevent investors or 'slumlords' from buying up homes. This keeps starter homes available for the target group. It is good for community spirit in Nederweert if owners live in their own homes.
statements.con: In addition to owner-occupied homes, there must be sufficient rental homes in the municipality of Nederweert. Because of this rule the number of rental homes is decreasing. People should have the freedom to rent out their house.

statements.title: The municipality should build a bicycle bridge over the Randweg Zuid.
statements.moreInfo: This concerns a bicycle bridge over Randweg Zuid at the height of the McDonald’s.
statements.pro: A bridge makes the traffic situation safer for cyclists. This ensures better traffic flow for cars on the N275.
statements.con: A bicycle crossing with traffic lights is sufficient for safety. A bicycle bridge is too expensive.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
statements.moreInfo: Farmers need a lot of land for their business. That land is usually their property, but they cannot do anything they want with it. The municipality determines the land use via a zoning plan. The designation of a piece of land can be, for example, housing, shops or agriculture. If a farmer wants to use land zoned for agriculture for something else as well, the municipality must change the zoning plan. This applies to all activities that are not agriculture, such as housing development, a farm shop, a campsite or a daycare.
statements.pro: Farmers should have the freedom to earn money in other ways as well. Such initiatives are good for tourism and the leisure economy in Nederweert.
statements.con: Nederweert does not need dozens of campsites or shops in the rural area. Not every location is suitable for everything. The municipality should assess this per location.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for poverty assistance. More people would then receive help.
statements.pro: Too many residents of Nederweert now live in poverty. Some people currently cannot participate in social and cultural activities because of a low income. That must be prevented.
statements.con: Poverty assistance is intended only for those who need it most. We should make existing poverty schemes better known. That will make more people use them.

statements.title: The municipality should not allow new lily cultivation on agricultural land.

titleParts.glossary: Growing lilies on agricultural land.

statements.moreInfo: Lilies are grown in several places in Nederweert. Pesticides are used against fungi and weeds. The municipality cannot currently prohibit these growers from cultivating lilies. For new locations the municipality can set rules, for example to ban lily cultivation near houses.
statements.pro: Harmful pesticides are used in the cultivation of lilies. That is bad for the environment. We could use agricultural land better for food production.
statements.con: We should only reduce the use of harmful pesticides in lily cultivation. A general ban on lily cultivation is too complicated.

statements.title: A group of residents selected by lottery should advise the municipality on important issues.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality can involve residents in decisions about important topics. One way to do this is through a citizens' assembly. In a citizens' assembly a group of residents is chosen by lottery. This group reflects society, for example in age and place of residence. The participants talk together about a topic and then give advice to the municipality. The municipal council ultimately decides what to do with that advice.
statements.pro: It is important to involve all residents in municipal decision-making in this way. Residents have many ideas and knowledge. The municipality can make use of that.
statements.con: We should involve residents in municipal government in other ways. There are already enough ways for residents to give advice, for example the village councils in all parts of Nederweert.

statements.title: In the area around the canal crossroads only businesses for recreation and tourism should be allowed.
statements.moreInfo: The canal junction at Nederweert is the intersection of the Wessem–Nederweert Canal, the Zuid-Willemsvaart and the Noordervaart. There are plans to build a container transshipment facility there: a place where containers are transferred from trucks to small ships. At the same time the scouting group has plans in this area for a new clubhouse with a play forest and a campsite.
statements.pro: The Kanalenviersprong is a unique natural area. There must definitely be no container terminal here.
statements.con: It is not good to fully fix the zoning of the canal crossroads at this moment.

statements.title: The speed limit outside built-up areas should be 60 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.pro: A 60 km/h speed limit makes traffic safer for pedestrians and cyclists. That leads to fewer traffic casualties. Nederweert should have the same rules as the surrounding municipalities; this speed limit already applies there.
statements.con: We should only address unsafe roads in the countryside, for example by making wider cycle paths. A general speed reduction in the rural area is bad for traffic flow and Nederweert’s accessibility.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.moreInfo: People with a low income often have difficulty paying municipal taxes. They can apply for exemption (kwijtschelding). Exemption means you do not have to pay the municipal taxes and levies, or part of them. The municipality decides whether you receive exemption. In Nederweert you can get exemption for municipal taxes and levies such as property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB), waste collection charges and sewerage charges.
statements.pro: We can provide people in poverty with more targeted assistance than with general waivers.
statements.con: Waiving taxes is a good way to combat and prevent poverty.

statements.title: The municipality should give farmers money to make their businesses more environmentally friendly.
statements.pro: The municipality should help farmers make their businesses future-proof so they can remain in Nederweert.
statements.con: Farmers should pay themselves for making their businesses more sustainable. Other entrepreneurs do the same.

statements.title: The municipality should cooperate in expanding the Brainport region, the tech area around Eindhoven.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meewerken aan de uitbreiding van de Brainportregio, de regio met

titleParts.glossary: Companies that mainly make technical products. Think of software, AI, and internet services.

statements.pro: This offers opportunities for the economy and employment for residents of Nederweert. The expansion cannot be stopped. Therefore Nederweert is best off participating in that development.
statements.con: We must preserve the identity and village character of the municipality of Nederweert. Nederweert should not grow by building extra houses for people who work in the Brainport region.

statements.title: The municipality should spend money on extra language lessons for people with limited language skills.
statements.pro: People can only participate in society once they speak Dutch. That prevents loneliness. Learning to read and write is important for finding work and being self-reliant.
statements.con: Learning the language is the individual’s responsibility. The municipality does not have to pay for it. There are already many activities in Nederweert to improve Dutch language skills.

statements.title: The wishes of the associations that use De Pinnenhof should guide the plans for the centre’s new construction.
statements.pro: The users of De Pinnenhof know best what they need for their activities.
statements.con: We should give equal consideration to the feasibility and affordability of plans.

statements.title: By 2050 Nederweert must no longer emit greenhouse gases.
statements.pro: The municipality has a duty to pursue this goal for future generations. Innovative solutions make this goal achievable-for example, storing energy from solar panels or wind turbines in a community battery.
statements.con: The municipality must remain realistic. Being emission-free by 2050 is not feasible. Rapid sustainability measures cause excessively high costs for residents.

statements.title: The municipality must give priority to local entrepreneurs when allocating sites on business parks.
statements.pro: Local entrepreneurs deserve some extra help to grow. This prevents the construction of large industrial halls that do not fit Nederweert's village character.
statements.con: It does not matter where entrepreneurs come from, as long as they fit with Nederweert. Fewer rules for entrepreneurs are good for Nederweert’s economy.

shootoutStatements.title: Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in our municipality from now on.
The municipality should designate more streets as bicycle streets.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Residents should be able to go to a single one-stop care desk for all healthcare organizations.
